compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can run the test suite without encountering v2 test paths or stubs
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Update or remove test files that reference v2 ticket system patterns (FAKE_TK stubs, TK_CALLS tracking, .tickets/ path fixtures, v2 dual-path test assertions). Tests for scripts modified in story 1 need their v2 test paths removed. Tests that exclusively test v2 behavior should be deleted.
**Why**: Tests must align with the v3-only code after stories 1-4 are complete.
**Scope**:
- IN: All 38 test files identified in the v2 reference inventory. Categories: (a) tests for modified scripts — update to match v3-only code (test-classify-task.sh, test-sprint-next-batch.sh, test-sprint-list-epics.sh, test-merge-to-main-qt4u.sh, test-merge-squash-rebase.sh, test-merge-to-main-cleanliness.sh, test-lifecycle-portability.sh, test-lock-acquire-ticket-format.sh, test-end-session-error-sweep.sh, test-commit-failure-tracker.sh), (b) tests with FAKE_TK stubs — replace with v3 ticket command stubs, (c) tests with .tickets/ fixtures — remove or replace with v3 fixtures, (d) tests that exclusively test v2 behavior — delete, (e) config tests (test-read-config.sh, test-flat-config-e2e.sh) — remove issue_tracker.create_cmd test lines
- OUT: Non-test files (handled by stories 1-4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, no test file contains FAKE_TK, TK_CALLS, TK_LOG variables or .tickets/ path fixtures. Satisfies: v2-specific test paths are removed
- When this story is complete, all existing tests pass after v2 removal. Satisfies: All existing tests pass after v2 removal
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] 38 test files affected — categorize each as update/remove before making changes
- [Reliability] Some tests may use FAKE_TK for testing ticket creation flow — these need v3 stubs, not just deletion
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: All 14 child tasks closed. Story complete.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
